test_name: Create and Execute Automated Test Case
description: This scenario tests the user's ability to create a new automated test case, execute it, and verify the results on the platform.
steps: Navigate to the Roost GPT CLI Form page.
Enter a test name in the 'Test Name' input field.
Enter a valid OpenAI token in the 'Open AI Token' input field.
Click the 'Unmask Token' button to verify the token visibility.
Select 'Cloud Git' as the repository type using the radio button.
Enter a valid GitHub token in the 'GitHub Source Token' input field.
Select all necessary test options such as 'Traverse to all sub-directories' and 'Check for Vulnerability' by interacting with respective labels.
Enter functions to test in the 'Functions to Test' input field.
Click the 'Download Env Config' button to download the configuration.
Navigate back to the Test Dashboard.
Click on the newly created test case from the list to open it.
Click the 'Run Test' button to execute the test.
Wait for the test execution to complete and check the results.
Review the test report generated after execution.
expected_results: User can successfully create a new test case with all necessary configurations.
Tokens are correctly unmasked and verified.
The environment configuration is downloaded successfully.
The test case executes without errors and results are displayed.
The test report accurately reflects the test execution.
edge_cases: Entering an invalid OpenAI token and verifying error handling.
Attempting to unmask a token when it's not entered.
Selecting both repository types and checking the system response.
Running a test case without selecting any functions to test.
Downloading the environment configuration without completing all fields.
data_requirements: Test case name that adheres to naming conventions.
Functions to test in the application.
dependencies: User must be logged in to access the test creation page.
The user must have valid API tokens for OpenAI and GitHub.
The application should have the necessary permissions to access the GitHub repository.
api_interactions: API calls to OpenAI for token verification.
GitHub API interactions for repository access.
Internal API calls for executing the test case and retrieving results.
performance_metrics: Time taken to download environment configuration.
accessibility_requirements: All form fields should be accessible via keyboard navigation.
Screen readers should announce the labels correctly.
Color contrast should meet WCAG 2.1 AA standards.
